changeReason: Original mistranslates お疲れ様 as 'congratulations'; new translation correctly renders it as 'well done,' which conveys the Japanese sense of acknowledging effort/completion.

TextENGNew: Did you enjoy the four worlds you traveled through?
changeReason: New translation specifies 四つの世界 as 'four worlds' instead of vague 'various worlds,' preserving the exact number referenced in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: Now then — this long, long story has at last come to its end.
changeReason: New translation captures ようやく ('at last/finally'), conveying the sense that the ending was long-awaited, which the original omits.

TextENGNew: There were honestly times when the sheer length of it all nearly wore me down... but now that the end is here, I find I'm a little reluctant to let go.
And so, for you who have passed through every last world — I have one final gift.
You may be a little surprised — but this is a small present, just from me to you.
Now then — will you return to the Books Menu for me...?
changeReason: Original uses collaborative 'shall we return' implying joint action; new translation correctly renders the Japanese request form (戻ってみてくれるかしら) as 'will you return for me,' making clear the speaker is asking the reader to act.
